title: Slivers
objectives: Build complex scroll effects with CustomScrollView

sections.markdown: A sliver is a scrollable area that knows how to render itself given a viewport's scroll position. Slivers compose inside a CustomScrollView to build advanced scroll effects (collapsing headers, mixed lists/grids).

ListView is actually a CustomScrollView with one SliverList under the hood. Slivers give you lazy building (only visible items are built) plus effects like collapsing/pinned headers that you can't do by nesting normal widgets.

Nesting scrollables badly (a ListView inside a Column inside another scroll) instead of composing slivers — causes unbounded-height errors or double scrollbars. Use one CustomScrollView with multiple slivers.
Use SliverChildBuilderDelegate (lazy) for long lists; reserve the children: [] form for short, known lists. Combine SliverPersistentHeader for sticky sections.

flashcards.back: A scrollable region that renders itself based on the viewport's scroll offset; composed in a CustomScrollView.

flashcards.front: What is ListView under the hood?
flashcards.back: A CustomScrollView with a single lazy SliverList.
It builds only visible items (lazy), saving memory and time for long lists.

quiz.explain: CustomScrollView hosts a list of slivers in one viewport.

quiz.q: Lazy building of list items uses…
quiz.explain: The builder delegate creates only visible children.

challenges.text: Build a CustomScrollView with a SliverAppBar + SliverList.
Mix a SliverGrid and SliverList in one scroll view.
Create a pinned sticky header with SliverPersistentHeader.
Build a collapsing parallax header with custom scroll behavior.

interviewQuestions: What is a sliver and why use it?
How does ListView relate to slivers?
How do you build a collapsing app bar?
How do you avoid nested-scroll problems?
